feed.title: John Biesnecker
feed.subtitle: The personal website of John Biesnecker.

entry.title: Who Are We, If Not Measured By Our Impact On Others?
entry.summary: Who are we, if not measured by our impact on others? That’s who we are! We’re not who we say we are, we’re not who we want to be – we are the sum of the influence and impact that we have, in our lives, on others.
entry.content:
Who are we, if not measured by our impact on others? That’s who we are! We’re not who we say we are, we’re not who we want to be – we are the sum of the influence and impact that we have, in our lives, on others.

– Carl Sagan

entry.title: #ShantyTok Explainer
entry.summary: Who knew 19th century whaling songs could be so fun?
entry.content:
If you, like the rest of world, are enthralled by the phenomenon that is #shantytok, you might enjoy this explainer video:

The Guardian also has a good piece on the story behind The Wellerman.

Wellerman’s six verses tell the epic tale of a ship, the Billy of Tea, and its crew’s battle – “for 40 days, or even more” – to land a defiant whale. With the struggle ongoing at the shanty’s end, “the Wellerman makes his regular call, to encourage the Captain, crew and all”.

Archer suggests that it is the shanty’s “cheerful energy and hopeful outlook” – in contrast to other more “dreary” whaling songs – that has led to Wellerman’s rediscovery on social media.

“My guess is that the Covid lockdowns have put millions of young [people] into a similar situation that young whalers were in 200 years ago: confined for the foreseeable future, often far from home, running out of necessities, always in risk of sudden death, and spending long hours with no communal activities to cheer them up.”

ShantyTok is exactly the sort of wholesomeness we all need right now.

entry.title: Hello Again, World
entry.summary: Dipping my toes back into blogging.
entry.content:
I haven’t maintained a blog for some time now, for no real reason at all except that I didn’t have the will to write, or really to do much of anything. It’s been a rough couple of years–not just COVID, but starting before that and amplified by COVID. I’m trying to dig my way out of that funk, and one of the ways I’m doing that is by starting to write again.

I have some things to write about–I’m learning Russian, and I have some side projects going here and there–and I might try to import some of the better posts from old blogs into this one. More than anything I want to get back into the habit of writing, as it’s one of the things that has kept me centered in the past.

Hope all of you are having a good 2021.
